lines.en_lit: comes to measure naphtha, says to him: Measure for yourself, comes to measure balsam, says to him: Wait for me until I measure with you, so that you and I will be perfumed.

the school of Rabbi Yishmael it was taught: Sin stupefies the heart of a person as it is stated: “And do not impurify yourselves with them, so that you should not be thereby impurified” Do not read “And be impurified [venitmetem]”; rather, And your be stupefied venitamtem].

The Sages taught “And do not impurify yourselves with them, so that you should not be thereby impurified”; a person who impurifies himself a bit, they impurify him greatly. below, they impurify him above, in this world, they impurify him in the World-to-Come.

the Sages taught “Sanctify yourselves and you will be sanctified” a person sanctifies himself a bit, they sanctify him greatly. below, they sanctify him above. in this world, they sanctify him in the World-to-Come.

MISHNA: would mix in the receptacle and draw two lots Upon one was written: For God. And upon one was written: For Azazel. The deputy to right, and the head of the patrilineal family to his left. If for the name came up in his right the Deputy would say to him: My master, High Priest, raise your right And if name came up in his left the head of the patrilineal family would say to him: My master, High Priest, raise your left

he would place upon the two goats, And he would say: For God, a sin-offering. Rabbi Yishmael says: He need not say: a sin-offering. Rather, For God. And respond after him: Blessed be the name of His glorious kingdom forever and all time.
GEMARA: Why do I mixed a receptacle In order that he not intentionally take
Rava said: receptacle was of wood and was unconsecrated, and it had enough only for two hands.

Ravina strongly objects to this: Granted, had enough only for two hands. in order that he not intentionally take But unconsecrated? Let it be consecrated If so, it would be a sacred vessel of wood, and we do not make a sacred vessel from wood. let it be made of silver let it be made of gold. the Torah spared the money of the Jewish people

The mishna is not in accordance with this tanna was taught Rabbi Yehuda says in the name of Rabbi Eliezer: The Deputy and the High Priest insert their hands into the receptacle. If comes up in the High Priest’s right the Deputy says to him: My master, High Priest, raise your right And if comes up in the right of the Deputy, the head of the patrilineal family says to the High Priest: Speak your word
Let the Deputy say to him. Since the lot did not come up in hand, he be discouraged
With regard to what do disagree? Sage, holds the Deputy’s right is preferable to the High Priest’s left And Sage, holds they are equivalent.

And who is this tanna who argues with Rabbi Yehuda? It is Rabbi Ḥanina, the Deputy of the priests, as it was taught Rabbi Ḥanina, the Deputy of the priests, says: Why Deputy at the right Because if some disqualification befalls the High Priest, the Deputy can step in and serve in his stead.

The Sages taught: forty years that Shimon HaTzaddik served the lot the right From then onward, sometimes it arose in the right sometimes it arose in the left the strip of crimson turned white, From then onward, it sometimes turned white sometimes it did not turn white. the western lamp would burn From then onward, it sometimes burned sometimes it went out.

And the fire on the arrangement kept going strongly, such that the priests did not need to bring wood to the arrangement except for the two logs in order to fulfill the mitzva of wood From then onward, sometimes kept going strongly sometimes it did not, and so the priests could not avoid bringing wood to the arrangement throughout the entire day.

And a blessing was sent upon the omer; and to the the two loaves and to the shewbread, And each priest that received an olive-bulk there were those ate it and were satisfied, and there were those ate of it and left over From then onward, a curse was sent upon the omer, and to the two loaves, and to the shewbread, each priest received size of a bean; the discreet, ones would withdraw their hands, and the voracious ones would take and eat And an incident occurred with one who took his portion and that of his fellow, and they called him: Son of
